sessions.intent: Five-pass simplicity/OBP review of the C# diff on fix-stepvartypes-incremental vs runtime2

before.assumptions: Branch name suggests an incremental stepvartypes fix, but the diff is broader (builder web UI, cost tracking, test report cycle, per-step timings, var-slot description). C# diff is narrow: OpenAi.cs cost math, Run.cs entity (rename CapturedOutput→Output, add Timings), test/run.cs (event-binding capture), modules/this.cs (drop trailing 'string' from %var% slot desc), BuildResponse.cs (comment), new Timing/Timings types.
before.risk: Long event-binding wiring in test/run.cs RunSingleAsync (~190 lines); hard-coded LLM pricing table; pre-existing System.IO in report.cs (out of scope for this branch — flagged on prior branch).

actions.context: Five-pass review (OBP rules + shape smells, simplification, readability, behavioral, deletion). No OBP violations introduced. Cost math validated against OpenAI usage semantics. Timings collection is textbook OBP shape. Pre-existing System.IO in report.cs noted but not gated on.

after.notes: 8-file C# diff is clean. One pre-existing System.IO debt in test/report.cs noted at top of report (not introduced on this branch — predates merge base). Low-priority readability suggestion to extract four event-binding blocks in test/run.cs RunSingleAsync if a fifth gets added later — not requested now.

sessions.intent: Post-merge re-validation of v1 PASS — branch was caught up with runtime2 (purge-systemio-from-actions merged in) plus a coder one-line fix

before.assumptions: v1 PASSed pre-merge. Merge brought in 65 C# files / 1525+ lines from purge-systemio-from-actions (already PASS-verdicted on its source branch) — not re-litigating that content. Re-validating the 8 v1-reviewed files against post-merge state and the coder's .ToString() fix at test/run.cs:163.
before.risk: Merge may have left dead code or mis-adapted patterns in the previously-reviewed files; coder fix may not be idiomatic.

actions.context: Post-merge re-validation. v1 findings still hold. v1 pre-existing System.IO debt in report.cs is CLOSED via merge (now uses path.@this.Resolve+WriteText). Coder's .ToString() fix at test/run.cs:163 is mechanically correct and idiomatic (matches GoalCall.cs:75, cache/wrap.cs:52). One LOW finding introduced by merge cleanup: dead local 'var app = Context.App;' at report.cs:40.
Codeanalyzer v2 outputs — PASS verdict with one LOW finding.

after.notes: Post-merge state is clean. One LOW: dead local at report.cs:40 (merge cleanup leftover, trivial delete). Optional micro: hoist new[] { '/', '\' } at report.cs:302 to a static readonly field. Not gating on either.

sessions.intent: Test-quality validation of the 8 branch-unique C# files; check whether tests verify intent or admit false greens

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v1/v2 both PASSed (code is OBP-clean). My job is whether the tests honestly verify the new behavior. Branch has no coder/ folder so no baseline-tests.md is available.
before.risk: Property rename CapturedOutput→Output may have stale callers in tests; new event-binding paths and OpenAI cost math are easy to test cheaply but easy to leave uncovered.

actions.context: Clean-rebuilt PlangConsole (OK); dotnet build PLang.Tests fails with CS1061 at ReportActionTests.cs:56 and EdgeCaseTests.cs:129 — coder did not propagate CapturedOutput→Output rename. C# suite unrunnable. PLang suite: 195 pass / 22 fail / 217 total via Tests/ + PlangConsole binary. No coder baseline to triage failures against. Found 9 issues: 1 build-break, 4 missing-coverage (output capture, Timings, cost math positive case, ResolveImage success), 1 weak-assertion (Contains vs IsEqualTo on the headline %var% fix), 3 minor/missing.

after.notes: Property rename without dependent updates broke the C# build; codeanalyzer PASSed without compiling. Headline %var% fix has a Contains() assertion that does not differentiate old from new. No baseline-tests.md so PLang regressions cannot be triaged. Coder needs to fix build, write coverage for the four new behaviors, tighten the slot-description assertion, and produce a baseline file going forward.
